definitions: An organic compound containing a hydroxyl group (-OH) attached to a saturated carbon atom, meaning all carbon-carbon bonds are single bonds.
A type of alcohol characterized by the absence of double or triple bonds within its carbon chain, making it fully saturated with hydrogen atoms.
examples: Ethanol is a common saturated aliphatic alcohol.
The synthesis involved a saturated aliphatic alcohol as a key intermediate.
Many industrial solvents are derived from a saturated aliphatic alcohol.
